raw_text:
```json
{
  "claim_texts": [
    "Restoring the Research and Development (R&D) Tax Credit and Net Operating Loss (NOL) deductions for Life Sciences companies in California can fuel innovation and create jobs.",
    "California life sciences companies developed more than 1,380 medicines that entered clinical trials in 2019.",
    "California's biopharmaceutical companies are making life-saving discoveries through research and development for COVID-19 treatments and vaccines.",
    "California's research and development tax credit incentivizes biotechnology companies to invest in R&D activities, leading to increased employment opportunities in science and engineering.",
    "Investments in life sciences generated more than $11 billion in new sales revenues and taxes for California and created over 56,000 new jobs.",
    "Gene and cell therapies are emerging as innovative technologies targeting oncology, central nervous system (CNS) disorders, and rare diseases.",
    "COVID-19 has contributed to growth in the life sciences sector, leading to the development of therapeutics and vaccines for this disease.",
    "The California life sciences ecosystem is a cycle involving academic research, venture capital investment, and the growth of successful companies.",
    "California is one of the largest biopharmaceutical innovation hubs in the world.",
    "California must provide tax stability to maintain its leadership status in biotechnology research and development jobs.",
    "Investing in diverse, collaborative teams is essential for developing transformative medicines for rare and rheumatic diseases.",
    "High-skilled research and development teams have produced anti-COVID vaccines rapidly."
  ]
}
```
